title: Non-inverting sensor amplifier into ADC
prompt: My analog sensor's output is too small to read accurately, so I want to amplify it with a non-inverting op-amp stage (LM358) and feed the amplified signal into my ESP32-C3 ADC. Wire the amplifier's output to an ADC pin and keep it inside 3.3 V. Firmware reads the amplified value. For the bench test the conditioned output sits around mid-scale (~1.65 V).

abstraction.reason: the LM358 has no simulatable SPICE subcircuit (validation blocks UNDEFINED_SPICE_MODEL), so the op-amp stage OUTPUT is modelled with a #105 voltage_source stand-in at the conditioned operating point
